findings.summary: STOP coverage is aggregate, not per-checkpoint: bypassing only the entry STOP check in run_ci_repair_attempt leaves all 3 driver tests green (the 4 downstream STOP checks compensate before any Codex/evidence/push step). Under that single-check bypass the repair_attempts slot is consumed and phase set REWORK despite active STOP. Full STOP-bypass (all 5 checks) IS caught by test_stop_file_prevents_ci_repair_routing. Non-blocking; suggest a follow-up test asserting repair_attempts is not consumed when STOP is present at entry.
CI repairs draw from the same repair_attempts budget as checks/review repairs; matches the spec's 'SAME bounded repair budget' wording, recorded so it is not later mistaken for a bug.
test_stop_file_prevents_ci_repair_routing does not assert the CI_REPAIR_SKIPPED_STOP event; it relies on absence of CI_REPAIR_ROUTED and zero codex calls.

mutation_tests.name: remove repair-budget check (start_attempt >= max_attempts dropped)
mutation_tests.observed: test_ci_failure_with_exhausted_budget_stops_with_ci_blocked FAILED (IndexError: CI-wait queue exhausted because repair routed past budget)

mutation_tests.name: bypass entry STOP check only (if False and stop_requested)
mutation_tests.observed: 3 passed; downstream STOP checks compensate before CI_REPAIR_ROUTED/codex

mutation_tests.name: bypass ALL 5 STOP checks in run_ci_repair_attempt
status_doctor core.bare=true FAIL downgraded to WARN
